name: R5SignatureElementMapToR4
title: Cross-version mapping for FHIR R5 Signature to FHIR R4 Signature
description: This ConceptMap represents cross-version mappings for elements from a FHIR R5 Signature to FHIR R4.

target.comment:
Element `Signature.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.type` as `Equivalent`.
Examples include attesting to: authorship, correct transcription, and witness of specific event. Also known as a "Commitment Type Indication".

Element `Signature.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.type` as `Equivalent`.
Examples include attesting to: authorship, correct transcription, and witness of specific event. Also known as a "Commitment Type Indication".

Element `Signature.when` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.when` as `Equivalent`.
This should agree with the information in the signature.

Element `Signature.when` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.when` as `Equivalent`.
This should agree with the information in the signature.

Element `Signature.who` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.who` as `Equivalent`.
This should agree with the information in the signature.

Element `Signature.who` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.who` as `Equivalent`.
This should agree with the information in the signature.

Element `Signature.onBehalfOf` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.onBehalfOf` as `Equivalent`.
The party that can't sign. For example a child.

Element `Signature.onBehalfOf` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.onBehalfOf` as `Equivalent`.
The party that can't sign. For example a child.

Element `Signature.targetFormat` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.targetFormat` as `Equivalent`.
"xml", "json" and "ttl" are allowed, which describe the simple encodings described in the specification (and imply appropriate bundle support). Otherwise, mime types are legal here.

Element `Signature.targetFormat` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.targetFormat` as `Equivalent`.
"xml", "json" and "ttl" are allowed, which describe the simple encodings described in the specification (and imply appropriate bundle support). Otherwise, mime types are legal here.
Element `Signature.sigFormat` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.sigFormat` as `Equivalent`.
Element `Signature.sigFormat` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.sigFormat` as `Equivalent`.

Element `Signature.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.data` as `Equivalent`.
Where the signature type is an XML DigSig, the signed content is a FHIR Resource(s), the signature is of the XML form of the Resource(s) using  XML-Signature (XMLDIG) "Detached Signature" form.

Element `Signature.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Signature.data` as `Equivalent`.
Where the signature type is an XML DigSig, the signed content is a FHIR Resource(s), the signature is of the XML form of the Resource(s) using  XML-Signature (XMLDIG) "Detached Signature" form.
